input.instruction: A weakener fired on a credibility evidence package. Using ONLY the supplied pattern definition and measures, explain what the flag means, why it fired, and what the engineer should do. Choose ONE action_class from the §5B vocabulary, report a verbalized confidence, and escalate if uncertain. Stay within measure-don't-judge: report on the evidence package, never on whether the model is correct.

fired_pattern.definition: Discretization convergence is demonstrated only to within a coarse band of the asymptotic value.

case_context.cou: the QoI is compared to a 5% acceptance tolerance

answer_key.gold_mechanism: The convergence study is monotone but the finest mesh is still ~8% (GCI) from the asymptote, exceeding the COU's 5% acceptance tolerance; the discretization error is not demonstrably below what the decision tolerates.

gold_action.posture_basis: standard-derived (ASME V&V 40: credibility bounded to the validated domain)

parameters.scope: finer-mesh convergence to within the 5% tolerance

forbidden_claims: asserts the QoI is wrong (a verdict the convergence gap does not establish)
treats a monotone trend as convergence to within the tolerance

hardness.obvious_signal: a mesh-convergence study is present and the trend is monotone toward an asymptote
hardness.conflicting_signal: the finest mesh in the study is still ~8% from the Richardson-extrapolated asymptote (GCI ~8%), and the COU's acceptance tolerance is 5% — the demonstrated convergence does not reach the tolerance the decision needs

label_provenance.external_grounding: ASME V&V 10/40 discretization: demonstrated convergence must reach the decision tolerance; a GCI above the acceptance tolerance is an unmet verification bar.

by_field.gold_action.selected_class: provisional-self-adjudicated (row-builder; needs independent expert/solver adjudication before disposition-gate use)
